---
title: "Assignment 1"
---

My first assignment has two parts. The first part also has two parts and the second part contains lots of tasks.

### (a) "Veri Bilimi ve Endüstri Mühendisliği Üzerine Sohbetler - Baykal Hafızoğlu & Erdi Daşdemir"

The video discusses the relationship between data science and industrial engineering and how these fields complement each other today. Data science is a field that aims to obtain meaningful results by analyzing large amounts of data and using these results in decision-making processes. Valuable information can be extracted from data through the combination of statistics, mathematics, and computer science. As technology advances and the amount of data increases rapidly, the need for experts who can analyze data is also increasing. The video also emphasizes the strong connection between data science and industrial engineering. Industrial engineers make decisions based on data analysis in areas such as production planning, inventory control, demand forecasting, and supply chain management. Therefore, data science methods help to build more accurate models and make systems work more efficiently. Thanks to methods such as statistical analysis, machine learning, and data mining, companies can make more accurate and faster decisions by using their data more effectively. Therefore, it is emphasized that data analysis and programming skills will become an important competency for industrial engineers in the future.

### (b) Open-Ended Question

Explain how data science contributes to decision-making processes in the field of industrial engineering and give examples of areas in which it can be used in businesses.

### Multiple Choice Question

Which of the following is NOT an application of data science in industrial engineering, according to the video?

A\) Demand forecasting

B\) Supply chain optimization

C\) Production planning

D\) Interpretation of historical events

***Correct answer: D) Interpretation of historical events***

```{r}
# Gerekli paketi ve veri setini içe aktarma
library(dslabs)
data("polls_us_election_2016") 

# Kendi bilgilerini tanımla (LÜTFEN AŞAĞIDAKİ BİLGİLERİ KENDİNE GÖRE DEĞİŞTİR)
my_first <- "ADIN" 
my_birth_year <- 2000 

# k değerini hesaplama
k <- (nchar(my_first) + my_birth_year) %% 15 + 8

# k'nın durumuna göre orijinal veri setinden satırları gösterme
if (k %% 2 == 0) {
  head(polls_us_election_2016, k) # Çift ise ilk k satır
} else {
  tail(polls_us_election_2016, k) # Tek ise son k satır
}

# Veri setindeki tüm NA değerlerinin toplamı
cat("Toplam NA sayısı (Orijinal): ", sum(is.na(polls_us_election_2016)), "\n")

# Sütun başına NA sayılarını büyükten küçüğe sıralayıp ilk 8'ini gösterme
na_per_column <- sort(colSums(is.na(polls_us_election_2016)), decreasing = TRUE)
head(na_per_column, 8)

# NA değerlerini istenen kurallara göre değiştirip yeni objeye atama
new_data <- polls_us_election_2016

for (col in names(new_data)) {
  if (is.numeric(new_data[[col]])) {
    # Sayısal sütunlar için: Doğum yılı + k
    new_data[[col]][is.na(new_data[[col]])] <- my_birth_year + k
  } else if (is.character(new_data[[col]]) || is.factor(new_data[[col]])) {
    # Karakter veya faktör sütunlar için: İsim + k
    if(is.factor(new_data[[col]])) {
      levels(new_data[[col]]) <- c(levels(new_data[[col]]), paste0(my_first, "", k))
    }
    new_data[[col]][is.na(new_data[[col]])] <- paste0(my_first, "", k)
  }
}

# Yeni veri setinden k satır gösterme
if (k %% 2 == 0) {
  head(new_data, k)
} else {
  tail(new_data, k)
}

# Yeni veri setinde kalan toplam NA sayısını gösterme
cat("Yeni veri setindeki toplam NA sayısı: ", sum(is.na(new_data)), "\n")

# Yeni veri setinde hiç NA kalıp kalmadığını kontrol etme (FALSE dönmelidir)
anyNA(new_data)
